---
title: "cleaning"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
```

CLEANING UP CLTUCH SIZE AND MEAN EGG SIZE
```{r}
#working with the egg counts of the five treatment groups
#read in the dataset
egg_counts <- read.csv(here("data", "raw data", "emergence_egg_counts(Sheet1).csv"))


#make sure egg counts are stored as numbers
egg_counts$green_eggs  <- as.numeric(egg_counts$green_eggs)
egg_counts$orange_eggs <- as.numeric(egg_counts$orange_eggs)

#################################################################################################################################
#read in the wild moths spreadsheet
wild_moths <- read.csv(here("data", "raw data", "wild moths spreadsheet.csv"))

#make sure the egg counts are stored as numbers
wild_moths$green_eggs <- as.numeric(wild_moths$green_eggs)
wild_moths$green_eggs <- as.numeric(wild_moths$green_eggs)

#clean up wild egg counts dataset
wild_moths_clean <- wild_moths %>%
  select(female_ID, green_eggs, orange_eggs, egg_area) %>%
  mutate(clutch_size = green_eggs + orange_eggs) %>%
  filter(!is.na(clutch_size)) %>%
  mutate(pupal_treatment = "wild", larval_treatment = "wild", sex = "F") %>%
  rename(adultID = female_ID, egg_mean_1A = egg_area)

###################################################################################################################################


#join the wild moth data onto the treatment group data
#clean up the dataset so it only has the females I am interested in
egg_counts_clean <- egg_counts %>%
  bind_rows(wild_moths_clean) %>%
  filter(sex == "F") %>% #only looking at females
  filter(!startsWith(adultID, "F")) %>% #remove sienna's females
  mutate(clutch_size = green_eggs + orange_eggs) %>% #clean up clutch size column
  filter(!is.na(clutch_size)) %>% #remove those with NA clutch size
  mutate(larval_treatment = str_extract(adultID, "^[^.]+")) %>% #make larval treatment column
  mutate( #fix these two rows because they were not assigned pupal treatment correctly
    pupal_treatment = case_when(
      adultID == "CTRL.WTH.152_P6_H" ~ "hot",
      adultID == "HOT.WTH.47_P2_A"   ~ "ambient",
      TRUE                          ~ pupal_treatment
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    larval_treatment = recode(
      larval_treatment,
      CTRL = "ambient",
      MEAN = "mean",
      COOL = "cool",
      WARM = "warm",
      HOT  = "hot",
      WTH = "wild"
    ))

```

```{r}
#now I want to add in the mean egg sizes
#read in the egg size spreadsheet
egg_size_results <- read.csv(here("data", "raw data", "mean egg sizes.csv"))

#make the clutch codes characters
egg_size_results$clutch_code <- as.character(egg_size_results$clutch_code)

###########################################################################################################################################################

#join the mean egg sizes onto the clutch sizes, using the clutch codes
results <- egg_counts_clean %>%
  left_join(egg_size_results, by = "clutch_code") %>% #join the egg size results on by clutch code
  mutate(egg_mean_1A = coalesce(egg_mean_1A.x, egg_mean_1A.y)) %>% #egg_mean_1A.x has the egg sizes of the wild moths, egg_mean_1A.y has the egg sizes of the treatment groups. I want to join these two columns together into one
  select(-egg_mean_1A.x, -egg_mean_1A.y, -treatment_combination, -notes.x, age, -age_at_photo, -uploaded, -dead, -cg, -prev_tray, -new_tray, -egg_mean_blinded, - egg_mean_round_three, -X.y) %>% #remove the columns that I don't need
  mutate(egg_mean_1A = coalesce(egg_mean_1A, replacement_image)) %>% #if I took a replacement image for a clutch, put this measurement into the egg_mean_1A column
  select(-replacement_image) %>% #now I can get rid of the replacement image column
  rename(egg_mean_1B = egg_mean_1B_same_set_of_10_eggs, egg_mean_2A = egg_mean_2A_different_set_of_10_eggs) %>% #rename these columns
  filter(!is.na(egg_mean_1A)) %>% #get rid of any rows that still don't have an egg size
  mutate(
    clutch_ID   = if_else(pupal_treatment == "wild", adultID, clutchID),
    clutch_code = if_else(pupal_treatment == "wild", adultID, clutch_code),
    maternalID = sub("^[^.]*\\.[^.]*\\.([^_]+)_.*$", "\\1", adultID))

results$sex <- as.character(results$sex)

```

```{r}
write.csv(results, file = here("data", "clean data", "results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```

CLEANING UP RAW EGG SIZES (OF EACH INDIVIDUAL EGG)

```{r}
#read in the raw egg sizes of the treatment groups
raw_egg_sizes <- read.csv(here("data", "raw data", "raw egg sizes.csv"))

#read in the raw egg sizes of the wild moths
wild_raw_egg_sizes <- read.csv(here("data", "raw data", "wild raw egg sizes.csv"))

#join together both spreadsheets
all_raw_egg_sizes <- raw_egg_sizes %>%
  bind_rows(wild_raw_egg_sizes)

#now join the raw egg sizes onto the nice, clean, results spreadsheet that we made above.
raw_egg_sizes_clean <- all_raw_egg_sizes %>%
   mutate(unique_egg_ID = paste0(clutch_code, "_", egg_number)) %>% #give each measurement a unique egg ID
  select(-mean_area) %>% #get rid of the mean area column - we don't need this
  left_join(results, by = "clutch_code") %>% #join the spreadsheets together
  filter(!str_detect(clutch_code, "_"))  %>% #remove any rows that have an _ in the clutch code - these are from the repeatability images.
  filter(!is.na(adultID))
```


```{r}
write.csv(raw_egg_sizes_clean, file = here("data", "clean data", "raw egg sizes.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```

CLEANING UP THE AMBIENT TEMPERATURE DATA

```{r}
#read in the dataset
ambient_data <- read.csv2(here("data", "raw data", "data_ambient_1_081225.csv"))

#rename the datetime column
names(ambient_data)[names(ambient_data) == "X2023.01.30.15.30"] <- "datetime"

#force it to be plaintext first
ambient_data$datetime <- as.character(ambient_data$datetime)

#cleanup weird characters
ambient_data$datetime <- gsub("-", " ", ambient_data$datetime)

#convert to datetime
ambient_data$datetime <- as.POSIXct(
  ambient_data$datetime,
  format = "%Y.%m.%d %H:%M",
  tz = "UTC"
)


#rename the temperature column
ambient_data <- ambient_data %>%
  rename(temperature = X21.06)

#now filter so that I am only looking at data from the first february 2025. for some reason, the temperatures in january seem way too high
#was the thermologger used for something else in january? was it indoors?
ambient_data_feb2025 <- ambient_data %>%
  filter(datetime >= as.POSIXct("2025-02-01 00:00:00", tz = "UTC")) %>%
  select(datetime, temperature)

#make sure temperature is saved as a numeric variable
ambient_data_feb2025$temperature <- as.numeric(ambient_data_feb2025$temperature)

write.csv(ambient_data_feb2025, file = here("data", "clean data", "ambient temperatures.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```
